title: paspalum limbatum
intro: Paspalum limbatum, also known as Buffalo Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, Asia, and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Paspalum limbatum include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Paspalum limbatum can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil to extract its medicinal properties.
